event: allogeneic HSCT from unrelated HLA-compatible donor
admitted to University Children's Hospital Tuebingen
morphine continuous infusion (max. 15 μg/kg BW per hour)
cerebral side effect of blinatumomab suspected
echocardiography showed multiple thrombi in ventricles
CT scans of thorax, abdomen, pelvis performed
multiple thromboembolic lesions with ischemia, bleeding, infarction
bone marrow aspiration showed aplasia with blasts
autopsy showed invasive R. pusillus mycosis
